# Santosh Sharma - First Website Launcher From Doti | Web Developer, Vlogger, and Thumbnail Designer

## About
Santosh Sharma is the First Website Launcher from Doti. He is a professional web developer, vlogger, and editor based in Doti, Nepal. He specializes in web development projects, gaming content, and video editing services. With a focus on quality and innovation, Santosh Sharma aims to provide engaging digital experiences for a diverse audience, while showcasing the vibrant culture and lifestyle of Nepal through vlogging.

**Areas of Work:** Web Development, Digital Media, Freelance Services, Brand Promotion Available

## Blog & Resources
### My Childhood
The pages of memories are still with me today in the courtyard of the mountain village, where the footsteps of my childhood are heard. My life played out in such a grim yet natural society, where there was only a touch of modernity. Electricity supply was uncertain, the roads were rough, made of stone and mud. But, despite those inconveniences, the warm friendliness of the people and the closeness to nature made my first world beautiful. The green hills of the village, the Kalkal Bug River, and the peaceful forest were the first teachers of my life. I learned the first lesson of my life when I met the game of stones in the same river and ran after the ox—simplicity is universally my beauty.

Today I clearly remember that day, when I first wore a blue shirt and black pants to school. A different bag on the shoulder, a copy in hand, and a strange curiosity growing in the mind! School was like our daily battle with friends. Sometimes the harsh discipline and beatings of the teachers scared my heart, but their love and affection for education became my future. The look of joy and pride on the teacher's face when I wrote the letter 'A' for the first time left an indelible impression on my childhood brain. From this, I understood—education is not just knowing letters; it is the distance from darkness to light. However, my days were bright; I left school during the work season and helped my parents with fairs. New clothes during Dashain could be the happiest time of the year. However, we were taught not to complain about those lacks, but to take infinite joy even in little things. The depth of those small joys is often overlooked in today's materialistic world.

People say that childhood is just a time to play and have fun. But in my case, there was also the silent contemplation time of my childhood. Even when playing with friends, I used to play alone. Sitting on the high peak of the mountain, I used to watch the birds flying in the sky and think—can I also go beyond the circle of this mountain and see the horizon beyond? I had a separate world in my mind—a world of dreams and plans. I used to dream of going to the city; I used to dream of changing my life and that of my family. Relatives and friends of the village said, "My dream is many things." It is said that it is safe to think of something without seeing it. But my inner self always kept telling me—"Small thinking only keeps a person in a narrow circle; your destination is the city beyond that and new possibilities." I resolved on this day—I will be the creator of my future.

When I turned 17, an emotional storm was brewing in my mind. On one side was the excitement of the future and on the other was the fear of uncertainty. I realized that life is no longer just about the innocent laughter of childhood. The time ahead is one of responsibility, hard decisions, and hard work.

I made a promise—"Even if my dream remains unfulfilled, I will never back down when I try." Mountain life would give me a priceless gift—the ability to struggle and patience. If the dream is fulfilled, that's another thing of mine, the courage to dream and the real meaning of life. Looking back now, dreams are not always 100 percent fulfilled. Father's sweat and mother's love—all these are the sources of my inner energy.

My teaching is the only truth—real success is not just personal happiness and convenience. In your ability to impact the lives of others, it is important to give back to your community and family. My unfulfilled dreams must make me tougher, more flexible, and compassionate.

The chapter on the mountain ended that day, but in it was the seed of my soul, which today is about to become a giant tree. My dream trip is underway. Life is neither uphill nor downhill on this highway, but I am not tired. I know that I am not strong as long as my struggles, desires, and dreams are alive. Those hills taught me only one thing—no matter how many clouds fall on me, the sun always shines. I am in search of the rising sun, and my journey continues forever...

Thus, the mountainous phase of my life is over, but the unfinished dream lives on now—a symbol of my inner energy, hope, and struggle. The journey of life is underway.
